name: My Paris delta - Marginal REVOLUTION
abstract: This article outlines observations from a return visit to Paris in 2023, highlighting notable changes since 2019. Key trends include an increase in Sunday shop openings, growing bilingualism in central Paris, a proliferation of diverse non-European restaurants, and the diminishing presence of city gas stations. Social shifts are also noted, such as a high prevalence of tattoos among young women and a significant decline in public smoking. The author also observes the availability of Rainier cherries in June, the continued strong presence of quality bookshops, and the discovery of new areas like the Bibliotheque Nationale, concluding that Paris is thriving.
